svg.text: ECO-SIM: Jatobá × Agouti - Zoochory & Scatter-Hoarding Clock
Late dry-season caching & heavy rain germination sync | RESEX Recanto das Araras

g.text: Jatobá pods are large, cement-hard. Few can
crack them; the Cutia (Agouti) has adapted
teeth. Dropped in dry season, the Cutia eats
some and buries the rest as caches across
the territory. Forgotten seeds, buried and
scarified, germinate when summer rains arrive.
Total Jatobá Seeds Hoarded: 16 pods cached
Without Agouti, seeds simply drop and rot.
This mutualism expands the forest, leveraging
memory lapses to ensure plant reproduction.
